routes.title: Emerson · Self-Reliance
routes.source: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Essays: First Series
routes.central_question: How can a person trust their own live perception instead of being ruled by society, reputation, or yesterday's self?
routes.why_it_hits: It answers the question of self-trust with compressed, forceful English: aphorism, command, reversal, and refusal to soften conflict.
routes.first_attention_act: Read one paragraph as an act of force: what enemy is Emerson naming, and what English phrase makes the blow land?

routes.title: Thoreau · Walden
routes.central_question: What remains when life is simplified until its real structure can be touched?
routes.why_it_hits: It turns simplification into an experiment rather than a slogan: reduce the noise, face the essential facts, then see what laws remain.
routes.first_attention_act: Track each verb of reduction: go, front, simplify, build, put foundations under.

routes.title: Douglass · Narrative
routes.source: Frederick Douglass, Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
routes.central_question: How does literacy turn from a skill into a weapon of freedom and self-possession?
routes.why_it_hits: It makes reading existential: words do not decorate life; they expose power, give thought a tongue, and make slavery mentally impossible.
routes.first_attention_act: Watch how a single sentence from the master becomes a revelation for the enslaved child.

routes.title: Shelley · Frankenstein
routes.source: Mary Shelley, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus
routes.central_question: What does creation demand from the creator, and what happens when knowledge outruns responsibility?
routes.why_it_hits: It speaks to ambition and making: the language first sounds luminous, then turns ethical, lonely, and accusatory.
routes.first_attention_act: Separate the intoxication of discovery from the obligation to the thing created.
